surface: rgoc CLI + generated Go artifacts + local HTTP probe

assertions.title: A representative CRUD fixture starts a localhost net/http server

steps.action: Compile the CRUD fixture to an evidence-local Go file and binary.
steps.expected: rgoc emits buildable Go using net/http and the generated binary can listen on 127.0.0.1:3111.
steps.observed: `http-server.go` imports `net/http`; `gofmt -d`, `go build`, and `go vet` all exited 0; the generated binary served `GET /items` on 127.0.0.1:3111 on the first readiness probe.

observedResults: Generated Go imported `net/http` and lowered to `http.NewServeMux`, `mux.HandleFunc`, and `http.ListenAndServe`.
The built binary accepted a live connection on 127.0.0.1:3111 and returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` with body `[]`.
commands: DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build nix develop -c dune exec rgoc -- /home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang/.factory/runtime/http-server.rg -o <evidence>/http-server.go

assertions.title: Port-in-use startup failure surfaces cleanly

steps.action: Occupy 127.0.0.1:3111 with a dummy listener, then start the generated server.
steps.expected: The generated server should fail immediately with a clear bind/listen error.
steps.observed: A Python dummy listener held 127.0.0.1:3111, then the generated server exited with status 2 and stderr `panic: listen tcp 127.0.0.1:3111: bind: address already in use`.

observedResults: The generated server did not hang or report success while the port was occupied.
The bind failure was explicit and user-visible in stderr.

assertions.title: GET /items returns the initial collection state

steps.action: Request `GET /items` against the started server before any create traffic.
steps.expected: The route should return the fixture's initial collection state.

observedResults: Initial collection state was empty as contracted by the fixture.

assertions.title: POST /items reads form data and returns an explicit create response

steps.expected: The server should read the form value, mutate state, and return a success response that reflects the submitted name.

observedResults: The response body echoed the submitted name instead of returning a fixed stub.

assertions.title: Read-after-create is observable over real HTTP

steps.expected: The created record should appear in the collection response.
steps.observed: The second `GET /items` returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` with body `["alice"]`.

observedResults: The state mutation from the create request was visible on a later read.

assertions.title: Unsupported methods are rejected with a stable non-2xx response

steps.action: Submit `DELETE /items` while the same server process is running.
steps.expected: The server should return a controlled non-2xx response without terminating.
steps.observed: `DELETE /items` returned `HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed` with body `method not allowed`, and later valid requests still succeeded against the same PID.

observedResults: Unsupported-method handling was stable and did not crash the process.

assertions.title: Malformed create requests return 400 and preserve state

steps.action: Submit `POST /items` without a `name` field, then read the collection again.
steps.expected: The server should return a client error and preserve the pre-error state.
steps.observed: `POST /items` with an empty body returned `HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request` and body `missing name`; the following `GET /items` still returned `["alice"]`.

observedResults: Malformed create traffic did not mutate stored items.

assertions.title: The full CRUD fixture survives error-path traffic and keeps serving valid requests

steps.action: Drive error-path traffic (`DELETE /items`, malformed `POST /items`) against the running CRUD server, then send later valid write and read requests to the same process.
steps.expected: The process should survive the error-path traffic and keep serving later valid requests.
steps.observed: After `DELETE /items` returned 405 and malformed `POST /items` returned 400, the same process later accepted `POST /items name=bob` with `201 Created` and a final `GET /items` returned `["alice", "bob"]`.

observedResults: The CRUD server remained healthy across both error paths and continued to serve valid traffic without restart.
The final collection state proved the same process remained mutable and responsive after the failures.

summary: Tested 8 assertions on the rgoc CLI / generated Go / local HTTP surface: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. The generated CRUD server compiled, passed gofmt/build/vet, handled required GET/POST/error flows, survived error-path traffic, and failed cleanly when port 3111 was already occupied.
